nodes.label: Save Data (.set)
nodes.note: LLM: Standard ending point for saving processed data.

nodes.label: Reject ICA Components
nodes.note:
⚠ Placeholder: 'placeholder' not in library
Paper: Independent component analysis (ICA) was used thereafter to detect and remove artifacts such as eye blinks and muscle movements. (2.5) ... Reject the artifact components. (Table 2) ... Only components with ≥90% probability of non-brain origin were removed. (2.5) This is a manual or semi-automated step after ICA and ICLabel.

nodes.label: ICLabel
nodes.note:
⚠ Placeholder: 'placeholder' not in library
Paper: The ICLabel plugin in EEGLAB was used to assist in component classification (2.5). This is an external plugin, not a standard EEGLAB function provided in the node list.

nodes.label: Run ICA
nodes.note: LLM: Independent component analysis (ICA) was used thereafter to detect and remove artifacts such as eye blinks and muscle movements. (2.5) ... Run ICA (independent component analysis) and reject components. (Table 2) No specific ICA algorithm or options were mentioned, so default parameters will be used.

nodes.label: Reject Bad Epochs
nodes.note:
⚠ Placeholder: 'placeholder' not in library
Paper: EEG preprocessing included standard procedures: ... epoch rejection. (2.5) This is typically a manual step not directly supported by a single node for automatic rejection.

nodes.label: Remove Baseline
nodes.note: LLM: Baseline normalization was applied using the −200 to 0 ms pre-stimulus interval. (2.6) ... All ERP waveforms were baseline-corrected using the pre-stimulus interval (−200 to 0 ms). (2.7)

nodes.label: Epoch
nodes.note: LLM: Wavelet analysis was conducted ... across epochs of −200 to +800 ms around stimulus onset. (2.6) Participants were instructed to respond just to oddball tones by pressing a key on a handheld keypad. (2.2) The specific event marker for oddball tones was not specified, so 'oddball' is used as a placeholder.

nodes.label: Interpolate Channels
nodes.note: LLM: EEG preprocessing included standard procedures: ... bad channel interpolation (2.5) ... Identify/reject bad channels: Detect and mark bad channels (e.g., noisy and flat) for removal or interpolation. (Table 2) Specific channels to interpolate were not mentioned.

nodes.label: Clean Rawdata
nodes.note: LLM: The raw data underwent visual inspection, with noisy channels either removed or interpolated. Time segments affected by significant artifacts were excluded. (2.5) ... Identify/reject bad channels: Detect and mark bad channels (e.g., noisy and flat) for removal or interpolation. ... Reject large artifact time points: Identify and reject time periods with large artifacts, such as muscle movements or eye blinks, that significantly distort the EEG signal. (Table 2) No specific criteria were mentioned, so default parameters will be used.

nodes.label: Filter
nodes.note: LLM: EEG preprocessing included standard procedures: ... high-pass filtering at 1 Hz (2.5) ... Apply a high-pass filter (1 Hz) to remove slow drifts and trends in the EEG signal. (Table 2)

nodes.label: Re-reference
nodes.note: LLM: Key steps involved ... re-referencing to a common average (2.5) ... Re-reference the EEG signals to a common average or specific reference channel to reduce noise and improve data quality. (Table 2)

nodes.label: Resample
nodes.note: LLM: After recording, the EEG data were resampled and converted to EEGLAB format for further processing. (2.4) The new sampling rate was not explicitly stated, so the default of 250 Hz is used.

nodes.label: Edit Channel Locations
nodes.note: LLM: Standard 10–20 caps were employed and adjusted based on individual head sizes. (2.4) ... load channel locations for better spatial interpretation. (Table 2)

nodes.label: Import Data (FileIO)
nodes.note: LLM: After recording, the EEG data were ... converted to EEGLAB format for further processing.

nodes.label: Create File Lists
nodes.note: LLM: Standard starting point for processing multiple files.
